_meta.source: ICRP Publication 119 (2012), Annex A, Table A.1
_meta.description: Effective dose coefficients for workers, simplified schema from ICRP Publication 119 Annex A, Table A.1.
notes: Top-level keys are radionuclides as listed in Table A.1.
Each radionuclide contains inhalation and/or ingestion data directly at nuclide level.
Some radionuclides in Table A.1 refer inhalation to Table B.1; those are stored with a note and only ingestion values here.
Mercury and sulphur include footnote-specific variants preserved in key names and notes.
Half-life strings are kept in the compact notation used in the table.
Inhalation data keep AMAD-dependent coefficients as e_1um and e_5um, matching Annex A.
Inhalation dose coefficients are given in Table B.1 for workers.
Inhalation dose coefficients are given in Table B.1 for workers.
Inhalation dose coefficients are given in Table B.1 for workers.
A dagger on ingestion f1/e indicates inorganic mercury in Table A.1.
A section sign on ingestion f1/e indicates organic mercury in Table A.1.
